document_title: Diploma in Teacher Education - Art and Craft Curriculum

sub_strands.name: 1.1 Meaning of Creative Arts
specific_learning_outcomes: Explain the meaning of creative arts
Identify components of creative arts: visual arts, music, dance, drama
Appreciate the value of creative arts in education
suggested_learning_experiences: Discussion on the definition and scope of creative arts.
View multimedia presentations showcasing examples from visual arts, music, dance, and drama.
Reflective writing on personal experiences with creative arts.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to explain the meaning of creative arts

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Comprehensively explains the meaning of creative arts
levels.Meets Expectation: Explains the meaning of creative arts
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially explains the meaning of creative arts
levels.Below Expectation: Only with support, explains the meaning of creative arts

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to identify components of creative arts: visual arts, music, dance, drama

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Accurately identifies all components of creative arts with examples
levels.Meets Expectation: Identifies components of creative arts
levels.Approaches Expectation: Identifies some components of creative arts
levels.Below Expectation: Only with assistance, identifies components of creative arts

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to appreciate the value of creative arts in education

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Clearly articulates the multifaceted value of creative arts in education
levels.Meets Expectation: Appreciates the value of creative arts in education
levels.Approaches Expectation: Partially appreciates the value of creative arts in education
levels.Below Expectation: Only with guidance, appreciates the value of creative arts in education

sub_strands.name: 1.2 The Interconnection of Creative Arts Components
specific_learning_outcomes: Illustrate the interconnection between visual arts, music, dance, and drama
Demonstrate how integrated arts enhance learning
suggested_learning_experiences: Create a performance piece that combines drawing (visual art), song (music), movement (dance), and storytelling (drama).
Analyze lesson plans or videos where multiple art forms are used together.
Group discussion on the benefits of an integrated approach to teaching creative arts.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to illustrate the interconnection of the components of creative arts in the Creating Strand

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Illustrates the interconnection of the components of creative arts in the Creating Strand in depth with valid examples
levels.Meets Expectation: Illustrates the interconnection of the components of creative arts in the Creating Strand
levels.Approaches Expectation: Illustrates the interconnection of the components of creative arts in the Creating Strand while missing out on few details
levels.Below Expectation: Illustrates the interconnection of the components of creative arts in the Creating Strand while missing out on a lot of details

sub_strands.name: 2.1 Basic Drawing Skills
specific_learning_outcomes: Demonstrate basic drawing skills: line, shape, form, space, texture
Use appropriate tools and materials for drawing
suggested_learning_experiences: Practice contour drawing, gesture drawing, and still-life sketching.
Experiment with pencils, charcoal, pastels, and ink.
Peer critique sessions focusing on use of elements of art.
core_competencies: Learning to learn and reflective practice

sub_strands.name: 2.2 Smudge Technique
specific_learning_outcomes: Illustrate smudging to create value/tone
Create a value gradation strip using smudging technique
Apply smudge technique in artwork
suggested_learning_experiences: Demonstration and practice of smudging with fingers, stumps, and cloth.
Create smooth transitions from light to dark on paper.
Incorporate smudging into a finished drawing of a simple object.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to illustrate smudging to create value/tone

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Creatively illustrates smudging to create value/tone
levels.Meets Expectation: Illustrates smudging to create value/tone
levels.Approaches Expectation: Illustrates smudging to create value/tone with few errors
levels.Below Expectation: Illustrates smudging to create value/tone with many errors

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to create value gradation strip using smudging technique

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Neatly creates value gradation strip using smudging technique
levels.Meets Expectation: Creates value gradation strip using smudging technique
levels.Approaches Expectation: Creates value gradation strip using smudging technique with minor errors
levels.Below Expectation: Creates value gradation strip using smudging technique with major errors

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to facilitate a lesson on smudge technique using scaffolding pedagogy

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Innovatively facilitates a lesson on smudge technique using scaffolding pedagogy
levels.Meets Expectation: Facilitates a lesson on smudge technique using scaffolding pedagogy
levels.Approaches Expectation: Facilitates a lesson on smudge technique using scaffolding pedagogy with few errors
levels.Below Expectation: Facilitates a lesson on smudge technique using scaffolding pedagogy with many errors

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to apply appropriate methods and tools for assessing smudge technique

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Innovatively applies appropriate methods and tools for assessing smudge technique
levels.Meets Expectation: Applies appropriate methods and tools for assessing smudge technique
levels.Approaches Expectation: Applies appropriate methods and tools for assessing smudge technique while missing out on minor details
levels.Below Expectation: Applies methods and tools for assessing smudge technique while missing out on major details

sub_strands.name: 2.3 Stippling Technique
specific_learning_outcomes: Create a value gradation strip by stippling to show light and dark effect
Apply stippling technique in artwork
suggested_learning_experiences: Practice making dots of varying density to create tone.
Create a stippled portrait or still life.
Compare stippling with other tonal techniques like hatching.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to create a value gradation strip by stippling to show light and dark effect

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Neatly creates a value gradation strip by stippling to show light and dark effect
levels.Meets Expectation: Creates a value gradation strip by stippling to show light and dark effect
levels.Approaches Expectation: Creates a value gradation strip by stippling to show light and dark effect with minor errors
levels.Below Expectation: Creates a value gradation strip by stippling to show light and dark effect with major errors

sub_strands.name: 2.4 Facilitating Drawing Lessons
specific_learning_outcomes: Facilitate a lesson using song on basic drawing activities
Facilitate lessons on different drawing techniques
suggested_learning_experiences: Design and deliver a micro lesson incorporating song to teach lines or shapes.
Use real objects or digital images as references for drawing exercises.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to facilitate a lesson using song on basic drawing activities

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Creatively facilitates a lesson using song on basic drawing activities
levels.Meets Expectation: Facilitates a lesson using song on basic drawing activities
levels.Approaches Expectation: Facilitates a lesson using song on basic drawing activities while missing out on few details
levels.Below Expectation: Facilitates a lesson using song on basic drawing activities missing out on many details

sub_strands.name: 3.1 Painted Decorative Effects on Fabric
specific_learning_outcomes: Apply painted decorative effect on fabric using varied tools
Use appropriate dyes and paints for fabric decoration
suggested_learning_experiences: Experiment with brushes, sponges, stamps, and spray bottles on cotton fabric.
Test different fabric paints and dyes for wash-fastness and texture.
Create a decorated textile piece (e.g., tote bag, apron).

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to apply painted decorative effect on fabric using varied tools

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Creatively applies painted decorative effect on fabric using varied tools
levels.Meets Expectation: Applies painted decorative effect on fabric using varied tools
levels.Approaches Expectation: Applies painted decorative effect on fabric using varied tools with minor errors
levels.Below Expectation: Applies painted decorative effect on fabric using varied tools with major errors

sub_strands.name: 3.2 Tie and Dye Techniques
specific_learning_outcomes: Apply varied tie and dye resist techniques to decorate a fabric
Demonstrate folding, binding, and stitching methods for resist dyeing
suggested_learning_experiences: Practice spiral, accordion fold, shibori, and stitch-resist techniques.
Use natural dyes (e.g., onion skins) and commercial dyes.
Compare results of different tying methods and dye concentrations.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to apply varied tie and dye resist techniques to decorate a fabric

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Applies varied tie and dye resist techniques to decorate a fabric skilfully
levels.Meets Expectation: Applies varied tie and dye resist techniques to decorate a fabric
levels.Approaches Expectation: Applies varied tie and dye resist techniques to decorate a fabric with minor errors
levels.Below Expectation: Applies varied tie and dye resist techniques to decorate a fabric with major errors

sub_strands.name: 4.1 Collage
specific_learning_outcomes: Create collage compositions for self-expression
Use appropriate adhesives and mounting techniques
suggested_learning_experiences: Collect and sort materials (paper, fabric, leaves, etc.) for collage.
Create thematic collages (e.g., identity, dreams, community).
Learn safe use of scissors, glue, and mounting boards.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to create a collage composition for self-expression

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Skilfully makes a collage composition for self-expression
levels.Meets Expectation: Creates a collage composition for self-expression
levels.Approaches Expectation: Creates a collage composition for self-expression with few errors
levels.Below Expectation: Creates a collage composition for self-expression with many errors

sub_strands.name: 4.2 Mosaic
specific_learning_outcomes: Facilitate mosaic lessons in pre-primary and primary levels
Use safe and appropriate materials for mosaics (e.g., paper, tiles, beads)
suggested_learning_experiences: Design and assemble a mosaic using colored paper or recycled materials.
Observe a demonstration on grouting techniques (if using tiles).
Plan and deliver a micro lesson on mosaic art for young learners.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to facilitate mosaic lessons in pre-primary and primary levels

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Flawlessly facilitates mosaic lessons in lower and upper primary levels
levels.Meets Expectation: Facilitates mosaic lessons in lower and upper primary levels
levels.Approaches Expectation: Facilitates mosaic lessons in lower and upper primary levels while missing on minor points
levels.Below Expectation: Facilitates mosaic lessons in lower and upper primary levels while missing on major points

sub_strands.name: 5.1 Assemblage
specific_learning_outcomes: Assemble materials to make a play item
Use found and recycled objects creatively and safely
suggested_learning_experiences: Collect natural and recycled materials (sticks, cardboard, bottles).
Construct three-dimensional forms like animals or vehicles.
Discuss the environmental benefits of upcycling in art.

sub_strands.name: 5.2 Carving
specific_learning_outcomes: Discuss strategies of facilitating carving in lower and upper primary
Make a form using carving technique
suggested_learning_experiences: Watch demonstrations on safe carving techniques using soap, soft wood, or clay.
Practice carving simple shapes under supervision.
Deliver a simulated lesson on introducing carving to children.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to discuss strategies of facilitating carving in lower and upper primary

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Articulately discusses strategies of facilitating carving in lower and upper primary
levels.Meets Expectation: Discusses strategies of facilitating carving in lower and upper primary
levels.Approaches Expectation: Fairly discusses strategies of facilitating carving in lower and upper primary
levels.Below Expectation: Hardly discusses any strategies of facilitating carving in lower and upper primary

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to make a form using carving technique

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Creatively and competently applies the technique of carving
levels.Meets Expectation: Applies the technique of carving competently
levels.Approaches Expectation: Fairly applies the technique of carving
levels.Below Expectation: Hardly applies the technique of carving

sub_strands.name: 5.3 Casting
specific_learning_outcomes: Prepare materials and tools for casting
Use papier mâché to make a form using casting technique
suggested_learning_experiences: Learn the process of mixing paper pulp and adhesive.
Create moulds using balloons or wire frames.
Build and dry papier mâché sculptures (e.g., masks, bowls).

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to prepare materials and tools for casting

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Consistently and correctly prepares materials and tools
levels.Meets Expectation: Correctly prepares materials and tools
levels.Approaches Expectation: Fairly prepares materials and tools
levels.Below Expectation: Hardly prepares the materials and tools

sub_strands.name: 6.1 Regular Repeat Patterns
specific_learning_outcomes: Discuss regular repeat patterns for printing and pattern making
Create prints using block printing, stamping, or stencil techniques
suggested_learning_experiences: Design a motif and transfer it onto a potato, rubber, or foam block.
Print repeating patterns on paper or fabric.
Experiment with color combinations and alignment.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to discuss regular repeat patterns for printing and pattern making

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Discusses regular repeat patterns for printing and pattern making in depth
levels.Meets Expectation: Discusses regular repeat patterns for printing and pattern making
levels.Approaches Expectation: Discusses regular repeat patterns for printing and pattern making while missing out on minor details
levels.Below Expectation: Discusses regular repeat patterns for printing and pattern making while missing out on major details

sub_strands.name: 6.2 Animation
specific_learning_outcomes: Discuss strategies of facilitating animation in upper primary
Make a flipbook animating a walking stick figure
suggested_learning_experiences: Study the principles of motion and frame-by-frame animation.
Create a flipbook showing a simple action (e.g., bouncing ball, walking).
Use digital tools (optional) to create short animations.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to discuss strategies of facilitating animation in upper primary

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Extensively discusses strategies of facilitating animation in upper primary
levels.Meets Expectation: Discusses strategies of facilitating animation in upper primary
levels.Approaches Expectation: Fairly discusses strategies of facilitating animation in upper primary
levels.Below Expectation: Hardly discusses any strategy of facilitating animation in upper primary

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to make a flipbook animating a walking stick figure

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Easily and accurately draws the sequence of the image, producing a consistent shift from one page to the next creating an illusion of movement. Uses sufficient frequency of the image, producing smoother movement
levels.Meets Expectation: Clearly draws the sequence of the image, producing a consistent illusion of movement. Uses adequate frequency of the image, producing smooth movement
levels.Approaches Expectation: Fairly draws the sequence of the image, producing an inconsistent illusion of movement
levels.Below Expectation: Struggles to draw the sequence of the image, failing to produce an illusion of movement

sub_strands.name: 7.1 Preparation of Materials
specific_learning_outcomes: Use appropriate preparation methods for soft and stiff materials
Ensure safety when preparing art materials
suggested_learning_experiences: Practice soaking paper for papier mâché, stretching canvas, or softening clay.
Learn how to condition different types of paint and adhesive.
Discuss storage and disposal of hazardous materials.

assessment_rubric.ability: Ability to use appropriate preparation methods for soft and stiff materials

levels.Exceeds Expectation: Skilfully uses appropriate preparation methods for soft and stiff materials
levels.Meets Expectation: Uses appropriate preparation methods for soft and stiff materials
levels.Approaches Expectation: Uses some inappropriate preparation methods for either soft or stiff materials
levels.Below Expectation: Uses inappropriate preparation methods for soft and stiff materials

sub_strands.name: 7.2 Tool Handling and Safety
specific_learning_outcomes: Demonstrate safe handling of art tools (scissors, knives, needles)
Maintain and store tools properly
suggested_learning_experiences: Practice passing scissors safely, using cutting mats, and storing sharp tools.
Inspect tools for damage and learn basic maintenance.
Develop classroom rules for tool use and sharing.

sub_strands.name: 8.1 Lesson Planning for Art and Craft
specific_learning_outcomes: Develop a lesson plan for an Art and Craft practical lesson
Integrate creativity, skill development, and safety
suggested_learning_experiences: Analyse sample lesson plans for structure and clarity.
Draft a lesson plan including objectives, resources, activities, differentiation, and assessment.
Peer-review lesson plans for feasibility and inclusivity.

sub_strands.name: 8.2 Demonstration Techniques
specific_learning_outcomes: Demonstrate art techniques clearly and safely
Sequence demonstrations logically for learner understanding
suggested_learning_experiences: Practice demonstrating a technique (e.g., tie-dye, smudging) step-by-step.
Use visual aids, models, and slow-motion actions.
Receive feedback on clarity and pacing of demonstrations.

sub_strands.name: 8.3 Assessment in Art and Craft
specific_learning_outcomes: Use diverse methods to assess creativity, skill, and effort
Provide constructive feedback on artistic work
suggested_learning_experiences: Design rubrics for specific projects (e.g., collage, sculpture).
Practice giving verbal and written feedback that is specific and encouraging.
Engage in peer assessment activities.

national_goals_of_education: Foster national unity, patriotism, and promote national cohesion.
Increase the level of knowledge of all people to enhance national capacity and strengthen the economy.
Affirm and promote the culture of all Kenyan people.
Provide education and training that enables learners to become self-reliant and contribute to national development.
Promote holistic well-being—physical, emotional, spiritual—for self-reliance.
Enable learners to recognize values, ethics, and morals from religious and cultural perspectives and integrate them with their environment.
Enable learners to use language efficiently as a communication, thinking, and learning tool.
Develop learners’ ability to seek, use, and disseminate information using communication technology.
Develop learners’ ability to solve problems using knowledge, skills, and attitudes.
Enable learners to recognize their potential and harness it for the benefit of family and society.
essence_statement: Education is a critical instrument for economic growth, national unity, and cultural affirmation. It aims to produce citizens capable of contributing to national development through knowledge, skills, and attitudes, while instilling values, ethics, and morals derived from religious and cultural heritage.
